rawContent: {"stage":"review","insights":[{"kind":"convention","text":"Code with surprising exclusions (like AdvancedContentEditor from read access) should include a clarifying comment at the exclusion site explaining the design rationale, not just the intent.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Multi-pass structured review (CRI-Triage → CRI-Detail → CRII-Context → CRIII-Holistic) catches both correctness issues and design-rationale gaps that single-pass reviews miss.","scope":"code-review"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Without an inline comment at the exclusion site itself, reviewers cannot distinguish intentional design from accidental omission — always comment the 'why' at the decision point.","scope":"auth"}]}
